sections.paragraph:
unused in Kal, pr. to flow, see Chald. נְגַר to draw out, and to flow; compare Heb מָגַר, and גָּרַר No. 1.

Niphal-

(1) to be poured out, to flow, used of water, 2Sa 14:14 used of the eye, Lam 3:49.

(2) to be stretched out (used of the hand), Psa 77:3. As to נִגָּרֹות, Job 20:28 see גָּרַר Niph.

Hiphil הִגִּיר-

(1) to pour out, Psa 75:9.

(2) to push down (stones from a mountain), Mic 1:6.

(3) figuratively to deliver (compare הֶעֱרָה to pour out, to deliver) in this phrase הִגִּיר פּ׳ עַל־יְדֵי חֶרֶב to deliver any one into the hands, i.e. into the power of the sword (compare עַל יְדֵי after verbs of delivering p. 331, A ); Eze 35:5 Jer 18:21 Psa 63:11 commonly ill rendered, to pour out by the hands of the sword.

Hophal הֻגַּר to be poured out, to be poured down, used of water, Mic 1:4.
